exports: Admin { resources(): ResourceInfo[]; list(table: string, opts?: ListOptions): Promise<AdminRow[]>; get(table: string, key: KeyInput): Promise<AdminRow | null>; create(table: string, input: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<unknown>; update(table: string, key: KeyInput, patch: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<unknown>; remove(table: string, key: KeyInput): Promise<unknown> }
class AdminError extends Error { code: AdminErrorCode }

types: SqlExecutor, AdminConfig, ResourceDeclaration, ReadsMap, ReadDescriptor, ColumnDescriptor, MutatorRegistry, Mutator, ResourceInfo, AdminRow, KeyInput, ListOptions, Admin, AdminErrorCode, ContractLike

invariants: Importing the part performs no I/O and never throws; every failure surfaces as a typed AdminError, and the raw driver error never escapes
Reads project ONLY the declared, non-redacted columns of a table declared in data_ownership.reads — a redacted or undeclared column is never selected or returned

A read or write against a table not declared in any installed part's reads fails with AdminError('unknown_resource') and issues zero SQL — there is no raw-table fallback, and a part declaring no reads contributes no resources

Admin writes never issue SQL: create/update/remove dispatch to the part's declared mutation export via the app-wired mutator; a table with no mutation for the action is read-only (AdminError('read_only')), and a mutator's own typed errors propagate unchanged so the part's invariants stay enforced where they live

Every identifier put into SQL (schema, table, column, order_by) is validated as a strict identifier and double-quoted, and every value is parameterized — a malformed reads declaration or a key carrying SQL metacharacters can never inject

admin.crud carries no compile-time or runtime dependency on the parts it administers — it operates purely off the passed reads declarations, the SqlExecutor seam, and the mutator registry, so it can administer a part it has no knowledge of
